title: Add ulimit annotation to kube gen & play
body:
Add a podman ulimit annotation to kube generate and play. If a container has a container with ulimits set, kube gen will add those as an annotation to the generated yaml. If kube play encounters the ulimit annotation, it will set ulimits for the container being played.

Fixes https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16404

Signed-off-by: Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>

<!--

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Add ulimit annotation to generated yaml file to be used by kube play.
```

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [umohnani8] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Bit confused with this PR @umohnani8 I don't believe there is any rlimit / ulimit support in Kubernetes? https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/3595

This is implementing something that doesn't exist in Kubernetes? Correct me if I'm wrong
We're guaranteed that ulimit/rlimit names and values don't include commas, right?

@cdrage this is solely for the podman side. Since users can set ulimits for podman containers they want to be able to apply ulmits for their workloads when using kube play. The ulimit annotation is only for podman to process and use during kube play.

@mheon from what I have looked up, I am pretty sure that ulimits don't include any commas, but @rhatdan do you know or can help confirm?
